/organizations/{organizationId}/policy-t.x-amf-description:
Policy Templates are the basis for creating new policies.
The platform provides out of the box with several policies and new
ones can be added by MuleSoft Support Team if needed.

get.description: Retrieve a list of policy templates.

406.description: The requested accept type is not supported.

get.description:
Retrieve specific details from the policy template, including the full
XML that is being injected as well as all of its configuration parameters.

404.description: There is no policy template defined under the specified id.

406.description: The requested accept type is not supported.

post.description: Creates a new application.

parameters.name: apiVersionId
parameters.description:
Used to associate the application with the IDP (Identity Provider) configured on the api instance.
This parameter is not needed when the organization is not fully migrated to crowd or when
the organization does not use a client provider.

parameters.name: groupInstanceId
parameters.description:
Used to associate the application with the IDP (Identity Provider) configured on the api instances of the group instance.
This parameter is not needed when the organization is not fully migrated to crowd or when
the organization does not use a client provider.

201.description: The new application has been successfully created.

400.description: The organization is federated and the apiVersionId query parameter is missing.

/organizations/{organizationId}/public/p.x-amf-description:
A collection of API portals within organization.
When performing a search by passing in 'query=foo' as a string, it is equivalent to passing in the following combination: 'query[name]=foo&query[version]=foo&query[description]=foo&query[tags]=foo&query[matchAll]=false'.

get.description: Returns a list of elements.

parameters.name: query[tags]
parameters.description: A list of keywords for matching API version tags in a search. Used in conjunction with other 'query[xxx]' to perform advanced search. Note: when any of 'query[xxx]' is used, the query parameter 'query' would be disregarded.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search criteria.

parameters.name: query[name]
parameters.description: A list of keywords for matching API name in a search. Used in conjunction with other 'query[xxx]' to perform advanced search. Note: when any of 'query[xxx]' is used, the query parameter 'query' would be disregarded.

parameters.name: query[version]
parameters.description: A list of keywords for matching API version name in a search. Used in conjunction with other 'query[xxx]' to perform advanced search. Note: when any of 'query[xxx]' is used, the query parameter 'query' would be disregarded.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Skip over a number of elements by specifying an offset value for the query.

parameters.name: deep
parameters.description: A boolean value that, when set, would cause the search be perform on the organization associated with 'organizationId' param, as well as all the sub-organizations under it - as long as the login user has permission to view the content.

parameters.name: ascending
parameters.description: Order for sorting.

parameters.name: query[description]
parameters.description: A list of keywords for matching API version description in a search. Used in conjunction with other 'query[xxx]' to perform advanced search. Note: when any of 'query[xxx]' is used, the query parameter 'query' would be disregarded.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The organization ID that the search should be run in. If not provided, search would be performed within the organization which the login user is under.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Property to sort by.

parameters.name: query[matchAll]
parameters.description: A boolean value that determines how other 'query[xxx]' values should work together in an advanced search When 'matchAll' is true, all other 'query[xxx]' would be chained with an 'AND' when performing a search, otherwise they are chained with an 'OR' Note: when any of 'query[xxx]' is used, the query parameter 'query' would be disregarded.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of elements on the response.

post.description: Creates a new element.

201.description: The new element has been successfully created.
